name: If a specific natural roll triggers an effect, is that effect still triggered if the dice is re-rolled and the re-roll gives a different result?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a specific natural roll triggers an effect, is that effect still triggered if the dice is re-rolled and the re-roll gives a different result?
answer: No. When you re-roll a dice, the previous roll is discarded. This includes any effects that roll may have triggered.

name: Can a Steam Tank's steam cannon fire grapeshot?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Steam Tank's steam cannon fire grapeshot?
answer: Yes. Grapeshot can be fired by any cannon (i.e., any weapon that fires using the 'Cannon Fire' special rule), as described on page 226 of the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

name: Models that are in base contact with different enemy units can choose which to attack. When is this decision made, at the start of the combat or when the model's turn to fight comes?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Models that are in base contact with different enemy units can choose which to attack. When is this decision made, at the start of the combat or when the model's turn to fight comes?
answer: When the model's turn to fight comes.

name: Do army special rules extend to any mercenaries I include?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do army special rules extend to any mercenaries I include?
answer: Unless stated otherwise, no.

name: If the Wounds characteristic of a war machine and its crew are different, which do I use to determine Unit Strength?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If the Wounds characteristic of a war machine and its crew are different, which do I use to determine Unit Strength?
answer: In such cases, use the Wounds characteristic of the crew.

name: Can a Wizard with the Warden of Saphery Elven Honour purchase magic armour?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wizard with the Warden of Saphery Elven Honour purchase magic armour?
answer: No.

name: A Wood Elf Noble that belongs to a Wild Rider Kindred may only wear light armour, but can they also wear a magic helmet?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: A Wood Elf Noble that belongs to a Wild Rider Kindred may only wear light armour, but can they also wear a magic helmet?
answer: Yes, a helmet can be worn in addition to light armour.

name: Do special rules conferred by a model's weapon apply to attacks made by that model's mount?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do special rules conferred by a model's weapon apply to attacks made by that model's mount?
answer: No. Any rules conferred by a weapon, be it magical or mundane, apply only to attacks made with that weapon. This can include, but is not limited to, rules unique to a specific type of weapon, special rules that apply to a particular weapon, or special rules that apply to a magic weapon. For example, if a Wizard is armed with the Sword of Swiftness, attacks made with that weapon have the Strikes First special rule, but the Wizard cannot claim that wielding the weapon allows them to cast Assailment spells at Initiative 10. To give another example, if a model has a special rule unique to its faction that grants additional rules to a hand weapon (the crew of an Orc Boar Chariot have the Choppas special rule, for example), that rule applies only to actual hand weapons carried by riders or chariot crew, not to weapons belonging to mounts or draft animals that 'count as' hand weapons.

name: Can a model that chooses to Stand & Shoot (or Fire & Flee) as a charge reaction attempt to cast a Magic Missile or Magical Vortex?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a model that chooses to Stand & Shoot (or Fire & Flee) as a charge reaction attempt to cast a Magic Missile or Magical Vortex?
answer: No. As stated on page 143, models that know such spells can attempt to cast them when chosen during the active player's Shooting phase. Perhaps obviously, this includes Bound spells.

name: Bearing two or more Obsidian Lodestones gives better Magic Resistance, but does an Obsidian Lodestone improve Magic Resistance granted by a different source?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Bearing two or more Obsidian Lodestones gives better Magic Resistance, but does an Obsidian Lodestone improve Magic Resistance granted by a different source?
answer: No.

name: Can a Warp Lightning Cannon fire grapeshot?
source_document: Online Rules Index Interpretation & Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Warp Lightning Cannon fire grapeshot?
answer: No. Grapeshot can only be fired by a cannon or weapon that fires using the 'Cannon Fire' special rule.

name: Can the Hagtree Fetish be used to re-roll rolls To Wound caused by a Bound Spell?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can the Hagtree Fetish be used to re-roll rolls To Wound caused by a Bound Spell?
answer: No. Bound Spells are cast by the item they are bound to, whilst Arcane Items such as the Hagtree Fetish affect spells cast by their bearer. Therefore, unless an item specifies it has an effect upon Bound Spells, it does not.

name: In a doubles event, as described in the Matched Play Guide, which armies, if any, can a Renegade Crowns army ally with?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: In a doubles event, as described in the Matched Play Guide, which armies, if any, can a Renegade Crowns army ally with?
answer: If the primary force is a Renegade Crowns, Empire of Man, or Kingdom of Bretonnia army, Renegade Crowns can be fielded as trusted allies.

name: Ogdruz Swampdigga has a Lore Familiar and knows spells from both the Lore of Elementalism and the Lore of Troll Magic. Can he choose his spells from both lores, or from just one?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Ogdruz Swampdigga has a Lore Familiar and knows spells from both the Lore of Elementalism and the Lore of Troll Magic. Can he choose his spells from both lores, or from just one?
answer: He can choose from both.

name: If a unit is limited to, for example, 0-3 per specific type of character, or 0-3 per 1,000 points, how many such units can I field in a 2,000 points army at an event using Combined Arms army composition?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit is limited to, for example, 0-3 per specific type of character, or 0-3 per 1,000 points, how many such units can I field in a 2,000 points army at an event using Combined Arms army composition?
answer: You can field up to three per character in the first example, and up to six in total in the second example. Combined Arms composition rules only restrict units with no other restriction on them. If a unit has any form of restriction, that takes precedence. Note that 1+, for example, is a restriction.

name: If a Beastmen Brayherds army includes an allied contingent of Beastmen, can the army contain two Herdstones, one in the main force and one in the allied contingent?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a Beastmen Brayherds army includes an allied contingent of Beastmen, can the army contain two Herdstones, one in the main force and one in the allied contingent?
answer: No. You can only include 0-1 Herdstone per army and, whilst an allied contingent may be in effect a small army within a larger army, in this regard you are ultimately playing with but one army. However, if two players partnered for a doubles game (as defined in the Matched Play Guide), they could each take a Herdstone.

name: When a Tomb Scorpion or unit of Necroserpents makes use of the Terrors Below special rule, is the number of Initiative tests the enemy unit takes equal to its own Unit Strength, or equal to the Unit Strength of the unit using the special rule?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: When a Tomb Scorpion or unit of Necroserpents makes use of the Terrors Below special rule, is the number of Initiative tests the enemy unit takes equal to its own Unit Strength, or equal to the Unit Strength of the unit using the special rule?
answer: The latter. This special rule represents an emerging unit displacing earth and dragging infantry to their doom. How much earth the unit can displace is determined by its own Unit Strength, not by that of the enemy.

name: Heavenly Beacon allows you to modify the roll by +1 or -1 when rolling to determine if Ambushers arrive. Is this cumulative? In other words, is it +2 or -2 if I have two Sky Lanterns?
source_document: Official Armies of Grand Cathay FAQ & Errata
question: Heavenly Beacon allows you to modify the roll by +1 or -1 when rolling to determine if Ambushers arrive. Is this cumulative? In other words, is it +2 or -2 if I have two Sky Lanterns?
answer: No, it's not cumulative. You may apply a +1 or -1 modifier if you have one or more Sky Lanterns.

name: Can you choose not to roll for the arrival of Ambushers during your Start of Turn sub-phase?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can you choose not to roll for the arrival of Ambushers during your Start of Turn sub-phase?
answer: No. Unless the unit itself or the scenario being played has a special rule that changes the way in which Ambushers arrive, you must roll for their arrival during each of your turns, from round two onwards. However, any special rules that affect the arrival of Ambushers which take effect after the Start of Turn sub-phase but before the Compulsory Moves sub-phase may be used as normal, regardless of the result of the Ambushers roll.

name: If a Wizard engaged in a challenge knows and uses an Assailment spell that can hit multiple enemy models (such as Assailment spells that use a template), are multiple enemy models hit, or is it only the other participant in the challenge that can be hit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Wizard engaged in a challenge knows and uses an Assailment spell that can hit multiple enemy models (such as Assailment spells that use a template), are multiple enemy models hit, or is it only the other participant in the challenge that can be hit?
answer: When an Assailment spell is cast in a challenge, it can only hit the other participant in the challenge.

name: The Hawk-eyed Archer rule allows a Waystalker to target any enemy character it can draw a line of sight to and to target specific models within an enemy unit. Does this apply to magic items that allow them to cast a Magic Missile?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: The Hawk-eyed Archer rule allows a Waystalker to target any enemy character it can draw a line of sight to and to target specific models within an enemy unit. Does this apply to magic items that allow them to cast a Magic Missile?
answer: No, the rule applies only to their Asrai longbow. Hawk-eyed they may be, but that does not mean they can snipe at enemy characters with powerful spells bound into magic items.

name: What happens if I wish to accept a challenge to fight a Warrior's Duel but do not have any single Wound champions in my army?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if I wish to accept a challenge to fight a Warrior's Duel but do not have any single Wound champions in my army?
answer: Roll off to determine who takes the first turn as normal.

name: Can Korhil Lionmane or a Chracian Chieftain join a unit of White Lions of Chrace?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can Korhil Lionmane or a Chracian Chieftain join a unit of White Lions of Chrace?
answer: Yes.

name: Can a Bretonnian army pray for the Blessing of the Lady if it includes an allied contingent?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Bretonnian army pray for the Blessing of the Lady if it includes an allied contingent?
answer: Yes, however an allied contingent of Bretonnians taken as part of any other army cannot pray. In order for Bretonnians to pray, the army they are part of must have the Blessings of the Lady rule, which only Kingdom of Bretonnia armies have.

name: Can the Infernal Puppet be used against a Bound spell?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can the Infernal Puppet be used against a Bound spell?
answer: Yes.

name: What constitutes a 'move'?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What constitutes a 'move'?
answer: Quite simply, if you touch a model or unit and change its position in any way, it has moved. There are some exceptions to this, a war machine that pivots does not count as having moved, for example, but in the vast majority of cases, the term 'move' follows the broadest definition; if a model or unit shifts its position or changes its location, it has moved.

name: The rulebook lists the minimum size of battlefield as 30" x 44" for games of up to 1,000 points, 44" x 60" for 1,001 to 3,000 points and 44" x 90" for games of 3,001 points or above. Do we have to use these sizes?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: The rulebook lists the minimum size of battlefield as 30" x 44" for games of up to 1,000 points, 44" x 60" for 1,001 to 3,000 points and 44" x 90" for games of 3,001 points or above. Do we have to use these sizes?
answer: No. These are the minimum recommended sizes. The normal size battlefield for a game of between 2,000 and 3,000 points is 48" x 72" (4' x 6') and players are encouraged to use this. The minimum sizes are based on the folding card battlefields Games Workshop produces. These are ideal when space is an issue or for smaller games. For example, we prefer a 44" x 60" battlefield for a game of 1,000 or 1,500 points, rather than the more traditional 48" x 48" battlefield. We find the smaller, rectangular shape more pleasing.

name: If I give armour runes to an Anvil of Doom, does the Anvil itself benefit, or just the crew?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If I give armour runes to an Anvil of Doom, does the Anvil itself benefit, or just the crew?
answer: Just the crew.

name:
How many spells can a Wizard attempt to cast during 
a single phase? For example, if a Wizard knows a Magical Vortex and a Magic Missile, can they attempt to cast both during the same Shooting phase?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question:
How many spells can a Wizard attempt to cast during 
a single phase? For example, if a Wizard knows a Magical Vortex and a Magic Missile, can they attempt to cast both during the same Shooting phase?
answer: A Wizard can only cast a number of spells up to their Level of Wizardry per turn, but can attempt to cast multiple spells during a single phase. In other words, should a Level 2 Wizard know two Magic Missiles and one Magical Vortex (three spells), they can only cast two of them per turn, but may cast both during the same phase.

name: Can my Battle Standard Bearer have a higher Leadership than my General?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can my Battle Standard Bearer have a higher Leadership than my General?
answer: No. As stated on page 203, the General is the character with the highest Leadership. If this means not upgrading the character with the highest Leadership in your army to be your Battle Standard Bearer so that they can be your General, so be it.

name: When a 'Dug In' unit of Skirmishers is charged by an enemy unit, does it lose that status?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: When a 'Dug In' unit of Skirmishers is charged by an enemy unit, does it lose that status?
answer: No.

name: Can a Necrosphinx make more than one attack each turn with its Decapitating strike?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Necrosphinx make more than one attack each turn with its Decapitating strike?
answer: No. The decapitating strike profile must be used as noted – to make one additional attack.

name: If a unit of Night Goblins that is engaged in combat still contains any unreleased Fanatics, can they be released whilst the unit is engaged?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit of Night Goblins that is engaged in combat still contains any unreleased Fanatics, can they be released whilst the unit is engaged?
answer: Provided they can be placed within 3" of their concealing unit and not touching the bases of any other models, yes.

name: If Harald Gemunsen issues a second challenge using his Skilled Duellist special rule, he can fight again immediately. Can his opponent in that challenge fight again?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If Harald Gemunsen issues a second challenge using his Skilled Duellist special rule, he can fight again immediately. Can his opponent in that challenge fight again?
answer: Yes. If Harald's second challenge is accepted it is fought immediately, meaning both Harald and his opponent can fight. However, Harald's mount may not fight, and nor may his opponent's.

name: Some attacks allow a specific model within a unit to be targeted. Can such an attack be used to break the coherency of a unit of Skirmishers and cause other models to be removed as well?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some attacks allow a specific model within a unit to be targeted. Can such an attack be used to break the coherency of a unit of Skirmishers and cause other models to be removed as well?
answer: No. As stated on page 184, you cannot remove a model from a unit of Skirmishers if doing so would cause the unit to lose coherency. However, if an attack targets a specific model (such as a champion or a character), that model must be removed if the attack reduces it to zero Wounds. In such cases, simply replace the removed model with another model belonging to the same unit, one that could be removed as a casualty, in order to maintain unit coherency.

name: If the spell Arcane Urgency is used to move a unit with Random Movement, can the unit move into contact with an enemy unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If the spell Arcane Urgency is used to move a unit with Random Movement, can the unit move into contact with an enemy unit?
answer: No. Whilst a unit with Random Movement can move into base contact with an enemy unit during the Compulsory Moves sub-phase, Arcane Urgency causes units to move when the spell is cast, during the Remaining Moves sub-phase.

name: Are units required to make Panic tests due to the actions of friendly models? For example, if a Wizard miscasts and rolls a 2-4 on the Miscast table, causing a Dimensional Cascade and inflicting sufficient casualties on a friendly unit to cause it to Panic, must that unit make a Panic test?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Are units required to make Panic tests due to the actions of friendly models? For example, if a Wizard miscasts and rolls a 2-4 on the Miscast table, causing a Dimensional Cascade and inflicting sufficient casualties on a friendly unit to cause it to Panic, must that unit make a Panic test?
answer: Yes (that example sounds to us like something that really would cause panic amongst the ranks!).

name: To defend a low linear obstacle, a unit must move its front rank into base contact with it. Are Skirmishers able to do this, or does their lack of ranks prevent it?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: To defend a low linear obstacle, a unit must move its front rank into base contact with it. Are Skirmishers able to do this, or does their lack of ranks prevent it?
answer: Skirmishers can defend a low linear obstacle. To do so, simply move as many Skirmishers as possible into base contact with the feature.

name: If my opponent and I have agreed to play a 2,000 points game but my army is not exactly 2,000 points (it's 1,997 points, for example), can I take two of something limited to 0-1 per 1,000 points by the army composition list I'm using?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If my opponent and I have agreed to play a 2,000 points game but my army is not exactly 2,000 points (it's 1,997 points, for example), can I take two of something limited to 0-1 per 1,000 points by the army composition list I'm using?
answer: Yes. Such limitations are based on the size of the game you are playing rather than the exact points value of your army.

name: Are the effects of the Grand Cannon's Thunderous Impact special rule cumulative? In other words, if a unit is within range of two strike points, does it suffer a -2 modifier to its Movement characteristic?
source_document: Official Armies of Grand Cathay FAQ & Errata
question: Are the effects of the Grand Cannon's Thunderous Impact special rule cumulative? In other words, if a unit is within range of two strike points, does it suffer a -2 modifier to its Movement characteristic?
answer: No, it's not cumulative.

name: Can a unit control more than one object on the battlefield? For example, can a unit control a special feature, a battlefield objective, and a secondary objective such as a baggage train, all at the same time?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit control more than one object on the battlefield? For example, can a unit control a special feature, a battlefield objective, and a secondary objective such as a baggage train, all at the same time?
answer: No. If a unit is in a position to control two or more such objects, you must choose one for it to control.

name: Can my army include a character that has a higher Leadership than my General if that character has a special rule (Loner, for example) that prevents them from being the General?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can my army include a character that has a higher Leadership than my General if that character has a special rule (Loner, for example) that prevents them from being the General?
answer: Yes.

name: If a Fanatic moves into contact with a unit that's engaged in combat, does it hit the unit(s) that unit is engaged with as well?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a Fanatic moves into contact with a unit that's engaged in combat, does it hit the unit(s) that unit is engaged with as well?
answer: When a Fanatic moves into contact with another unit, it continues in a straight line until it can be placed back on the battlefield. Any units that line passes through are hit. Units the line does not pass through are not hit.

name: If I take a Black Orc Warboss as the General of a Nomadic Waaagh!, can I take a unit of Boar Boys as a Core choice?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If I take a Black Orc Warboss as the General of a Nomadic Waaagh!, can I take a unit of Boar Boys as a Core choice?
answer: No. A unit of Boar Boys can only be taken as a Core choice if your General is an Orc Warboss.

name: How does the Dark Coven army special rule from the Wild Herd Army of Infamy interact with the Plague Chalice?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: How does the Dark Coven army special rule from the Wild Herd Army of Infamy interact with the Plague Chalice?
answer: In cases such as this, both outcomes can apply. If a natural double is rolled, the spell is cast as a perfect invocation and the caster suffers a hit from the Plague Chalice. However, the caster and any other members of their coven will then also suffer the effects of a roll on the Miscast table, as per the Dark Coven special rule.

name: Does a unit with the Blood Greed special rule always roll only a single D6 when making a Pursuit roll, or only whilst Frenzied?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Does a unit with the Blood Greed special rule always roll only a single D6 when making a Pursuit roll, or only whilst Frenzied?
answer: Only whilst Frenzied.

name: Does the Armour Bane (X) special rule apply to spells cast by a model with the rule, such as Assailment spells, Magic Missiles or Magical Vortexes?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does the Armour Bane (X) special rule apply to spells cast by a model with the rule, such as Assailment spells, Magic Missiles or Magical Vortexes?
answer: No.

name: Can a cannon target a point on the ground if woods or a hill lie between it and that point?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a cannon target a point on the ground if woods or a hill lie between it and that point?
answer: No, a cannon cannot shoot over woods or hills, even if it is on a hill. However, a stone thrower can if it chooses to fire indirectly.

name: The crew of Tomb Guard chariots are equipped with shields. Does this improve the Armour Value of the model from 4+ to 3+?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: The crew of Tomb Guard chariots are equipped with shields. Does this improve the Armour Value of the model from 4+ to 3+?
answer: No. A chariot's given Armour Value takes into account such equipment.

name: If a unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast  is obliged to declare a charge (if the unit is Frenzied or Impetuous, for example), must it do so? If so, can it make a charge move?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast  is obliged to declare a charge (if the unit is Frenzied or Impetuous, for example), must it do so? If so, can it make a charge move?
answer: Earthen Ramparts does not prevent a unit from declaring a charge, it prevents it from charging. In other words, yes the unit must declare a charge if obliged to do so. However, because the unit cannot charge, it does not move at all and the charge is failed.

name: If I mark the position of a unit then proceed to move it before putting it back where it was and moving it again, does this count as a take-back?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If I mark the position of a unit then proceed to move it before putting it back where it was and moving it again, does this count as a take-back?
answer: Yes, absolutely.

name: Can the champion in a unit of Royal Clan Warriors or Hammerers put weapon runes on a gromril great axe or great hammer?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can the champion in a unit of Royal Clan Warriors or Hammerers put weapon runes on a gromril great axe or great hammer?
answer: No. Weapon runes can only be inscribed upon a hand weapon or great weapon, as defined in the Warhammer: the Old World rulebook. The Royal Clan Warriors' gromril great axes and the Hammerers' great hammers are unique types of weapon, each with their own profile and rules.

name: Some monsters have weapons with notes that state they must make, or may choose to make, one attack, or one additional attack, with that weapon in combat. Can they make more than one attack with such weapons?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some monsters have weapons with notes that state they must make, or may choose to make, one attack, or one additional attack, with that weapon in combat. Can they make more than one attack with such weapons?
answer: No. If it is noted on a weapon's profile that the model may or must make a specific number of attacks with that weapon, normal attacks or otherwise, that's how many attacks it makes with that weapon.

name: Does a magic standard that affects a unit's weapons (the Razor Standard, for example) also make those weapons magical, giving them the Magical Attacks special rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a magic standard that affects a unit's weapons (the Razor Standard, for example) also make those weapons magical, giving them the Magical Attacks special rule?
answer: Not unless the description of the magic standard in question states that the weapons of a unit carrying it gain the Magical Attacks special rule. Generally speaking, purchasing a magic item doesn't conjure up additional bonuses from the æther.

name: Can a Wood Elf Realms army that includes an allied contingent place an additional wood using the Woodland Ambush rule?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wood Elf Realms army that includes an allied contingent place an additional wood using the Woodland Ambush rule?
answer: Yes. Note however that an allied contingent of Wood Elves taken as part of another army cannot.

name: Are the effects of the Casket of Souls' Bound spell 'Light of Protection' cumulative?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Are the effects of the Casket of Souls' Bound spell 'Light of Protection' cumulative?
answer: No.

name: Can a Steam Tank's Engineer Commander and steam cannon both shoot during the same turn?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Steam Tank's Engineer Commander and steam cannon both shoot during the same turn?
answer: Yes. The Engineer Commander doesn't fire the steam cannon, imagine there's a (very hot and bothered) crew hidden away inside the Steam Tank whose endeavours leave the Engineer Commander free to fire their own weapon.

name: Can a model use a Breath weapon after marching?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a model use a Breath weapon after marching?
answer: No. A model cannot shoot during the Shooting phase if it marched during the preceding Movement phase and, although they work differently to most missile weapons, Breath weapons are a type of missile weapon.

name: How do I determine the centre of a unit of Skirmishers?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How do I determine the centre of a unit of Skirmishers?
answer: The centre is the point that is equally distant from the models at the extremities of the group. However, when considering blast templates, the purpose of placing a blast template over the centre of a unit is to ensure a significant number of models are underneath it. In the case of a unit of Skirmishers, this is not always possible. Therefore, you may place the central hole of a blast template over the model closest to the centre of a unit of Skirmishers.

name: Does the Martial Prowess special rule allow models to make supporting attacks with weapons that do not have the Fight in Extra Rank special rule?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Does the Martial Prowess special rule allow models to make supporting attacks with weapons that do not have the Fight in Extra Rank special rule?
answer: No. Martial Prowess grants the ability to make supporting attacks to the flank or rear, as well as to its front. It does not grant the ability to make supporting attacks to models not normally able to do so.

name: Units must make a Panic test when a nearby friendly unit with a Unit Strength of 5 or more is destroyed. When is Unit Strength counted – from the start of the turn, the start of the phase, or the point at which the unit is destroyed?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Units must make a Panic test when a nearby friendly unit with a Unit Strength of 5 or more is destroyed. When is Unit Strength counted – from the start of the turn, the start of the phase, or the point at which the unit is destroyed?
answer: From the start of the phase during which the unit was destroyed. For example, if a friendly unit with a Unit Strength of 10 is destroyed by enemy shooting during a single Shooting phase, it causes panic in nearby friendly units. Players are not expected to remember the Unit Strength of every unit from turn to turn or phase to phase, but should keep track of Unit Strength during each phase.

name: If a unit that has 'Dug In' becomes engaged in combat, does it lose that status if a model within it accepts or declines a challenge and moves through the ranks?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit that has 'Dug In' becomes engaged in combat, does it lose that status if a model within it accepts or declines a challenge and moves through the ranks?
answer: No.

name: What is the furthest a model can move?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What is the furthest a model can move?
answer: No model can move more than twice its Movement characteristic or, when movement is determined by a dice roll (such as when a unit charges or flees), further than the distance rolled. There are, of course, exceptions to this, the most obvious being when a unit in Marching Column marches at triple its Movement characteristic, when models are moved by the effect of a spell, when a unit is obliged to continue its move rather than end up 'on top' of another unit, and obligatory moves (such as when a charging unit wheels to align with the enemy, or when a unit pivots before fleeing). Note that when a unit wheels, those at its rear do not really follow a long, curving path, as it might appear when moving a large block of models on the table. In reality, those models would take a more direct route, following the path of the front rank and resuming their formation behind it. Accordingly, we do not measure the distance moved by models in the rear ranks of a wheeling unit. Note also that, when a unit is permitted a free manoeuvre before or after moving, this is free in that it does not count towards the move itself, but the limitation that no model can move more than twice its Movement characteristic applies within the manoeuvre.

name: A unit of Empire Road Wardens may replace its crossbows with braces of pistols for +1 point per model. If it does so, must the Captain (champion) pay an additional +2 points to do the same?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: A unit of Empire Road Wardens may replace its crossbows with braces of pistols for +1 point per model. If it does so, must the Captain (champion) pay an additional +2 points to do the same?
answer: No. If the entire unit replaces its crossbows with braces of pistols, that includes the Captain. If the unit is armed with crossbows, the Captain has the option to replace theirs with a brace of pistols or a repeater pistol.

name: When deploying armies, must units be placed completely within their deployment zone?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When deploying armies, must units be placed completely within their deployment zone?
answer: Yes.

name: Can a character with the Shield of the Lady special rule retire to the rear of their unit after issuing a challenge, enabling them to avoid their opponent's attacks?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a character with the Shield of the Lady special rule retire to the rear of their unit after issuing a challenge, enabling them to avoid their opponent's attacks?
answer: No. Once a challenge has been issued and accepted neither combatant can retire until the challenge is resolved. Chivalry demands it.

name: When fighting a Warrior's Duel challenge between two unit champions, are such things as magic standards carried by their units counted?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: When fighting a Warrior's Duel challenge between two unit champions, are such things as magic standards carried by their units counted?
answer: No. A Warrior's Duel is fought between the two participants alone, without any support from their units.

name: Casting the same Enchantment or Hex spells on a unit more than once has no further effect, but what about spells with a range of 'Self' that affect all units within a certain distance of the caster?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Casting the same Enchantment or Hex spells on a unit more than once has no further effect, but what about spells with a range of 'Self' that affect all units within a certain distance of the caster?
answer: Unless stated otherwise, such spells are not cumulative and casting them multiple times will have no further effect.

name: If one participant in a challenge causes Impact Hits or makes Stomp Attacks, where are they directed?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If one participant in a challenge causes Impact Hits or makes Stomp Attacks, where are they directed?
answer: Impact Hits are resolved prior to issuing challenges. Stomp Attacks may be directed against the other participant in the challenge.

name: If a unit's fighting rank is engaged with two enemy units, one of which is a lone character engaged in a challenge, can models within the unit's fighting rank ignore the character even if they are closer to it than to the other enemy unit, or are their attacks lost?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit's fighting rank is engaged with two enemy units, one of which is a lone character engaged in a challenge, can models within the unit's fighting rank ignore the character even if they are closer to it than to the other enemy unit, or are their attacks lost?
answer: Models not engaged in a challenge cannot direct their attacks against models that are. In other words, any models that would normally have to direct their attacks against the character are free to direct them instead against the other unit their unit is engaged with.

name: If a character with the Mark of Nurgle (for example) is mounted on a chariot with the Mark of Chaos Undivided, does the chariot benefit from the character's Mark, or do I have to pay the points to give the chariot the Mark of Nurgle for it to gain the same benefits?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a character with the Mark of Nurgle (for example) is mounted on a chariot with the Mark of Chaos Undivided, does the chariot benefit from the character's Mark, or do I have to pay the points to give the chariot the Mark of Nurgle for it to gain the same benefits?
answer: If you want a chariot mount to have the same benefits from a Mark of Chaos that its rider has, you have to pay the points to give the chariot the Mark of Chaos.

name: Models cannot cast Magic Missiles or Magical Vortexes after marching, but does this include Bound spells?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Models cannot cast Magic Missiles or Magical Vortexes after marching, but does this include Bound spells?
answer: Yes.

name: Does the Daemon Heart increase its user’s Dispel Range?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Does the Daemon Heart increase its user’s Dispel Range?
answer: No.

name: Mastery of the Elemental Winds can only be used once per turn, but is this once per Wizard or once per army?
source_document: Official Armies of Grand Cathay FAQ & Errata
question: Mastery of the Elemental Winds can only be used once per turn, but is this once per Wizard or once per army?
answer: Once per army.

name: When a model fights, must it make all of its attacks, or can it choose to make fewer?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a model fights, must it make all of its attacks, or can it choose to make fewer?
answer: If a model is able to fight, it must make all of its attacks.

name: If my army includes a Mercenary character, can I make them my General?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If my army includes a Mercenary character, can I make them my General?
answer: No. An army’s General must be one of the characters listed in the 'characters' section of the army composition list you are using.

name: The Bow of Loren allows a Wood Elf character to make a number of shots equal to their Attacks characteristic. How does this interact with Enchanted Arrows?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: The Bow of Loren allows a Wood Elf character to make a number of shots equal to their Attacks characteristic. How does this interact with Enchanted Arrows?
answer: When firing an Enchanted Arrow it is assumed the model is firing only once, as is the norm. Therefore, one of the shots fired from the Bow of Loren can be made using an Enchanted Arrow, the other shots are resolved using ordinary (i.e., not Enchanted) arrows.

name: Can a model armed with two hand weapons choose to fight with just one hand weapon?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a model armed with two hand weapons choose to fight with just one hand weapon?
answer: Yes. As noted on page 213, unless stated otherwise, all models are assumed to be equipped with a hand weapon. A model with two has, by definition, two and may choose to fight with just one, foregoing the Extra Attacks (+1) special rule in favour of some other benefit.

name: Can a unit with Defensive Stakes be hit by Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks by a charging enemy?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit with Defensive Stakes be hit by Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks by a charging enemy?
answer: Yes. Whilst the stakes might do tremendous damage to 'cavalry' and 'chariots', the momentum of such models when they charge will carry them into the archers, although they may be turned to kindling in the attempt.

name: In a Nomadic Waaagh!, I can take one Black Orc Boss per Black Orc Chariot. If I take a Black Orc Boss mounted on a chariot, does that count?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: In a Nomadic Waaagh!, I can take one Black Orc Boss per Black Orc Chariot. If I take a Black Orc Boss mounted on a chariot, does that count?
answer: No. You can take one Black Orc Boss per Black Orc Chariot taken as a Core or Rare choice. It does not matter what the Boss is mounted on.

name: Does an allied contingent have to abide by the restrictions given in the army composition list it is drawn from?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does an allied contingent have to abide by the restrictions given in the army composition list it is drawn from?
answer: Yes. An allied contingent is a small army within a large army, made using a Grand Army or Army of Infamy composition list. Within a 2,000 points army, for example, an allied contingent of 500 points would have to spend at least 125 points on Core units, could spend no more than 250 points on characters, and would be unable to include any units limited to 0-1 per 1,000 points.

name: If a model has Armour Bane (X) listed amongst its special rules (i.e., if the model has the special rule itself rather than a weapon it carries having it), does it apply to Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a model has Armour Bane (X) listed amongst its special rules (i.e., if the model has the special rule itself rather than a weapon it carries having it), does it apply to Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks?
answer: Yes.

name: Sometimes, when a unit of Skirmishers charges or is charged, some of the models cannot move far enough to form up with the rest of the unit. What happens to those models?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Sometimes, when a unit of Skirmishers charges or is charged, some of the models cannot move far enough to form up with the rest of the unit. What happens to those models?
answer: The unit loses coherency, as described on page 184. This means models belonging to the unit must be removed from play as casualties until the unit becomes coherent.

name: If one participant in a challenge is killed, can other models engaged in the same combat direct their Attacks against the survivor during the same Combat phase when it is their turn to fight?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If one participant in a challenge is killed, can other models engaged in the same combat direct their Attacks against the survivor during the same Combat phase when it is their turn to fight?
answer: No. Even if one participant in a challenge has been slain, the challenge is considered to be ongoing until the end of the current Combat phase.

name: How are Victory Points scored for a Night Goblin unit that includes one or more Fanatics once its Fanatic(s) have been released?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: How are Victory Points scored for a Night Goblin unit that includes one or more Fanatics once its Fanatic(s) have been released?
answer: Fanatics are an upgrade for the unit; their cost is included when working out VP for the unit itself. In other words, the death of an individual Fanatic is not worth any VP, but destroying the unit it was concealed within will earn you the VP for the Fanatic as part of the unit's points cost.

name: If a character joins a unit of Skin Wolves, can they be transfigured by the Warped Form special rule?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a character joins a unit of Skin Wolves, can they be transfigured by the Warped Form special rule?
answer: No.

name: What happens if a Hit is rolled on the Scatter dice for an object that moves in a random direction, do I use the small arrow on the Hit icon to determine the direction?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a Hit is rolled on the Scatter dice for an object that moves in a random direction, do I use the small arrow on the Hit icon to determine the direction?
answer: Essentially, objects that move in a random direction scatter, as described on page 95. Such objects only move in the direction shown by the small arrow on the Hit icon if the rules tell you they do so. Otherwise, the object does not move.

name: If a Wizard has a magic item or special rule that allows them to re-roll a failed Casting roll, can they re-roll a natural double 1 and avoid a Miscast?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Wizard has a magic item or special rule that allows them to re-roll a failed Casting roll, can they re-roll a natural double 1 and avoid a Miscast?
answer: No. A roll of a natural double 1 isn't merely a failed Casting roll, it is a Miscast, as described on page 109.

name: Can a unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast make a Counter Charge charge reaction?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast make a Counter Charge charge reaction?
answer: No.

name: Before a Steam Tank charges, can it use the Steam Power special rule to increase its Movement?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Before a Steam Tank charges, can it use the Steam Power special rule to increase its Movement?
answer: No. The Steam Power special rule can be used by a Steam Tanks, a model that cannot march, instead of marching. In other words, in instances where most models or units would increase their movement by marching, a Steam Tank can instead make use of the Steam Power special rule. Charging is not such an instance.

name: Can a cannon target a point on the ground if another unit lies between it and that point?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a cannon target a point on the ground if another unit lies between it and that point?
answer: Not unless that cannon is on a hill and the unit is not. However, as above, a stone thrower can if it chooses to fire indirectly.

name: Can the wearer of the Wizarding Hat cast spells whilst wearing armour?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can the wearer of the Wizarding Hat cast spells whilst wearing armour?
answer: Yes. The wearer of the Wizarding Hat is not actually a Wizard – their magical powers are granted by a haunted hat which is not affected by any armour the model may wear.

name: Can a Casket of Souls move at the end of the Shooting phase of its turn using the Reserve Move special rule (should it have it)?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Casket of Souls move at the end of the Shooting phase of its turn using the Reserve Move special rule (should it have it)?
answer: Yes. Whilst a Casket of Souls is an Immovable Object, this special rule clearly states that it prevents the model moving during the Remaining Moves sub-phase. It does not prevent movement made at other times/during other phases.

name: When a model with the Slayer, Slayer of Daemons or Slayer of Dragons special rule hits an enemy unit using the Deathblow special rule, does that hit benefit from the Slayer special rule?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: When a model with the Slayer, Slayer of Daemons or Slayer of Dragons special rule hits an enemy unit using the Deathblow special rule, does that hit benefit from the Slayer special rule?
answer: Yes.

name: Can a unit that rallies and reforms during the Rally Fleeing Units sub-phase move during the Movement phase?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit that rallies and reforms during the Rally Fleeing Units sub-phase move during the Movement phase?
answer: Yes.

name: What happens if a scatter roll causes any part of an object to cross beyond the edge of the battlefield?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a scatter roll causes any part of an object to cross beyond the edge of the battlefield?
answer: The object is removed from play.

name: Can the wearer of the Wizarding Hat also wear a magic helmet, such as the Bedazzling Helm?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can the wearer of the Wizarding Hat also wear a magic helmet, such as the Bedazzling Helm?
answer: Yes, because one is an enchanted item, the other is a type of magic armour, but we hope anyone equipping a model in such a way would make the effort to depict it wearing two hats!

name: Is it possible to resurrect models in a unit of Skirmishers?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Is it possible to resurrect models in a unit of Skirmishers?
answer: Yes, simply place the resurrected models in coherency (i.e., within 1" of another model) with the rest of the unit.

name: Models in a fighting rank cannot fight if they are killed before they have a chance to fight. But can models in a second fighting rank (in the case of units within the Press of Battle rule) fight if the model in front is slain, and can a model make a supporting attack if the model(s) in the fighting ranks(s) in front of it are slain?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Models in a fighting rank cannot fight if they are killed before they have a chance to fight. But can models in a second fighting rank (in the case of units within the Press of Battle rule) fight if the model in front is slain, and can a model make a supporting attack if the model(s) in the fighting ranks(s) in front of it are slain?
answer: Yes. Unless they have been killed, models that are in a fighting rank or that are able to make supporting attacks can do so as normal. In other words, casualties inflicted reduce, firstly, the number of models in the fighting rank(s) that are able to fight (starting with the first fighting rank) and, secondly, the number of models in the 'supporting rank' that are able to fight.

name: Can a unit that is partially on a hill shoot in two ranks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit that is partially on a hill shoot in two ranks?
answer: No. To claim the benefits of being on a hill, a unit must be entirely on the hill.

name: How does a unit of Skirmishers form up when charged by two or more different enemy units from different directions?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How does a unit of Skirmishers form up when charged by two or more different enemy units from different directions?
answer: The Skirmishers should form up against the first charging unit to move into contact with them. The remaining enemy units then charge the formed Skirmishers. If any units are unable to complete their charge moves, their charges are failed.

name: What happens if a unit in Lance formation that declares a charge finds its movement blocked by the presence of an enemy unit to its flank, where the unit gets wider?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a unit in Lance formation that declares a charge finds its movement blocked by the presence of an enemy unit to its flank, where the unit gets wider?
answer: Such situations are covered by the rules for Accidental Contact During a Charge. If the Lance can wheel around the enemy unit, it should do so. If not, the Lance has two choices; halting its charge or continuing ahead and making contact with the enemy unit, treating it as a redirected charge.

name: Can a unit move whilst locked in combat?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit move whilst locked in combat?
answer: No.

name: Can a Black Orc Boss take a Black Orc Chariot as a mount in an army made using the Orc and Goblin Tribes Grand Army composition list?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Black Orc Boss take a Black Orc Chariot as a mount in an army made using the Orc and Goblin Tribes Grand Army composition list?
answer: They cannot. With heavy hearts, we must inform everyone who asked that that option is only available in armies made using the Nomadic Waaagh! Army of Infamy composition list.

name: If a unit that is subject to any Leadership modifiers uses the General's Inspiring Presence rule (or any other rule that allows a unit to use the Leadership of a nearby character), does the unit ignore those modifiers?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit that is subject to any Leadership modifiers uses the General's Inspiring Presence rule (or any other rule that allows a unit to use the Leadership of a nearby character), does the unit ignore those modifiers?
answer: No, the modifier will still apply. For example, if a unit loses a round of combat to a Terror causing enemy, the -1 modifier to Leadership caused by Terror applies to whichever Leadership characteristic the unit makes its Break test against, be that its own or that of a nearby character.

name: Some mounted models have the Counter Charge special rule but others do not, even though they have the same type of mount. Is this intentional?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some mounted models have the Counter Charge special rule but others do not, even though they have the same type of mount. Is this intentional?
answer: Yes. Counter Charge is a rule that represents the skill and ferocity of the rider, not their mount. In other words, not every rider is willing or able to perform a counter charge, regardless of their mount.

name: Can a cannon be shot in such a way as to hit an enemy unit that is engaged in combat with a friendly unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a cannon be shot in such a way as to hit an enemy unit that is engaged in combat with a friendly unit?
answer: No. Page 143 of the rulebook makes it clear that, except in rare cases, units cannot shoot enemy units that are engaged in combat. In the case of cannon, this means the target point cannot be placed in such a way as to risk the cannonball hitting a friendly unit, or an enemy unit engaged in combat with a friendly unit.

name: Can a missile weapon be used in combat?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a missile weapon be used in combat?
answer: No.

name: If the musician and/or standard bearer in a unit of Slayers are models that have been upgraded to Giant Slayers, what happens if they are slain, is their instrument and/or standard picked up by another Giant Slayer or by a Troll Slayer?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If the musician and/or standard bearer in a unit of Slayers are models that have been upgraded to Giant Slayers, what happens if they are slain, is their instrument and/or standard picked up by another Giant Slayer or by a Troll Slayer?
answer: It rather depends on the models that remain in the unit. If any Giant Slayers remain, they will pick up the instrument or standard, replacing the slain models like for like. If no Giant Slayers remain, a Troll Slayer will take their place. Note also that Giant Slayers are not unit champions. Thus in a unit of Slayers, the standard bearer would be the last model removed.

name: If a unit charges an enemy unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast, must it follow all the rules for charging an enemy unit behind a defended low linear obstacle, even though there is no such physical terrain between the two units?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit charges an enemy unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast, must it follow all the rules for charging an enemy unit behind a defended low linear obstacle, even though there is no such physical terrain between the two units?
answer: Yes. A unit upon which Earthen Ramparts has been cast counts as being behind a defended low linear obstacle when charged. Think of the spell’s effect as representing a magically manifested obstacle, rather than a physical terrain feature.

name: Do the points I spend on Core units within an allied contingent count towards the minimum percentage of Core units overall?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do the points I spend on Core units within an allied contingent count towards the minimum percentage of Core units overall?
answer: No.

name: Is Overkill counted by or against a champion in a challenge, or does it only count for characters?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Is Overkill counted by or against a champion in a challenge, or does it only count for characters?
answer: Overkill counts in all challenges, whether the participants are characters or champions.

name: If a spell has two Casting Values, do I have to say which I'm attempting to cast the spell at before making my Casting roll?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a spell has two Casting Values, do I have to say which I'm attempting to cast the spell at before making my Casting roll?
answer: No, your casting result determines the 'strength' of the spell. In other words, the higher your casting result, the more power the spell will have and the greater its effect will be.

name: Are unsaved wounds caused by the mount of a model bearing the Laurels of Victory worth 2 combat result points?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Are unsaved wounds caused by the mount of a model bearing the Laurels of Victory worth 2 combat result points?
answer: No, only unsaved wounds caused by the bearer (the rider) count.

name: What happens to a Fanatic that moves into or through the front arc of a unit of Bretonnian Peasant Bowmen equipped with 'defensive stakes'?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: What happens to a Fanatic that moves into or through the front arc of a unit of Bretonnian Peasant Bowmen equipped with 'defensive stakes'?
answer: Nothing good. Defensive stakes function very much like linear terrain, therefore a Fanatic that moves into contact with a row of defensive stakes, from any direction, comes to a very sudden stop and is removed from play as a casualty.

name: Do runes inscribed upon a Doomseeker’s whirling blades of death apply to Impact Hits?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Do runes inscribed upon a Doomseeker’s whirling blades of death apply to Impact Hits?
answer: Yes. As stated under Whirlwind of Death, Impact Hits caused by a Doomseeker are resolved using the profile of its whirling blades of death, which includes any runes the weapon is inscribed with.

name: When a unit Counter Charges, it rolls D3+1 to determine how far it moves. Is this roll counted as a Charge roll?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit Counter Charges, it rolls D3+1 to determine how far it moves. Is this roll counted as a Charge roll?
answer: No.

name: Is a unit's maximum possible charge range the maximum distance from an enemy unit at which it can declare a charge, or the maximum distance it can move when making a charge move?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Is a unit's maximum possible charge range the maximum distance from an enemy unit at which it can declare a charge, or the maximum distance it can move when making a charge move?
answer: It is the maximum distance from an enemy unit at which it can declare a charge.

name: Do Skirmishers have flank or rear arcs whilst engaged in combat?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do Skirmishers have flank or rear arcs whilst engaged in combat?
answer: No.

name: If a Remains in Play spell with two Casting Values is cast at the higher value, which is used when trying to dispel it during the Conjuration sub-phase?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Remains in Play spell with two Casting Values is cast at the higher value, which is used when trying to dispel it during the Conjuration sub-phase?
answer: As stated on page 111, in order to dispel a Remains in Play spell during your Conjuration sub-phase, you must beat the minimum casting value given in the spell's description. This is the case even if the spell was cast at the higher of two given casting values.

name: Two characters are engaged in a challenge whilst their units battle around them. At the end of the round, the losing unit might Fall Back in Good Order or Give Ground. If their enemy pursues or follows up, does the challenge continue?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Two characters are engaged in a challenge whilst their units battle around them. At the end of the round, the losing unit might Fall Back in Good Order or Give Ground. If their enemy pursues or follows up, does the challenge continue?
answer: Yes. As stated on page 211, if both participants in a challenge survive the round and if the combat continues, the challenge continues.

name: How does a bolt thrower work when shooting at a unit in Lance formation?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: How does a bolt thrower work when shooting at a unit in Lance formation?
answer: The number of models hit is based on the number of ranks overall. For example, a Lance of six knights (arranged in ranks  of one, two and three models) would suffer three hits. When a Lance is shot in its flank by a bolt thrower, the number of hits is based on the widest file. To continue the example above, three models would be hit based on the depth of the unit.

name: Can a Doomseeker join a unit of Slayers?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Doomseeker join a unit of Slayers?
answer: No, as per the errata, a Doomseeker is not a character. They are loners and outcasts even amongst Slayers and, as such, act as a unit of one model.

name: If a unit bearing a standard inscribed with the Master Rune of Hesitation Falls Back in Good Order and is pursued and caught by an enemy unit, does the rune take effect?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit bearing a standard inscribed with the Master Rune of Hesitation Falls Back in Good Order and is pursued and caught by an enemy unit, does the rune take effect?
answer: Yes. When a pursuing unit catches an enemy unit that Fell Back in Good Order, it counts as having charged that unit.

name: Can any type of cannon fire grapeshot?
source_document: Online Rules Index Interpretation & Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can any type of cannon fire grapeshot?
answer: Only cannons or weapons that fire using the 'Cannon Fire' special rule may also fire 'Grapeshot', as described on page 227 of the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook. This is in addition to any other special rules or additional modes of fire the weapon may have.

name: If a Wizard wears the Wizarding Hat, does it increase their Level of Wizardry?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Wizard wears the Wizarding Hat, does it increase their Level of Wizardry?
answer: No. As stated in the item's description, the wearer of the Wizarding Hat is a Level 1 Wizard and knows one randomly generated spell. This is clearly a huge detriment to a powerful Wizard, but might be a boon to any character that is not a Wizard.

name: Can a unit on a hill draw a line of sight across and shoot over another unit on the same hill?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit on a hill draw a line of sight across and shoot over another unit on the same hill?
answer: A unit that is closer to the top of a hill (i.e., the centre of the hill or the edge of the battlefield) can draw a line of sight across and shoot over one that is closer to the bottom. Units cannot draw a line of sight across or shoot over units that are closer to the top of a hill than they are.

name: Can the spell Spectral Doppelganger from the Lore of Illusion be used with a magic weapon that allows the wielder to make only a single attack?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can the spell Spectral Doppelganger from the Lore of Illusion be used with a magic weapon that allows the wielder to make only a single attack?
answer: No. Weapons limited to a single attack can only inflict a single hit.

name: How many attacks can a model with a split profile make if it is in the fighting rank but not in base contact with the enemy?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How many attacks can a model with a split profile make if it is in the fighting rank but not in base contact with the enemy?
answer: A model with a split profile consists of not one model, but several, all sharing the same base. Therefore, each model on that base could make a single attack. In the case of a cavalry model, for example, this would be one attack from the rider and one from their mount.

name: If a model bearing the Laurels of Victory Stands & Shoots as a charge reaction, causing one or more unsaved wounds, are those unsaved wounds each worth 2 combat result points?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a model bearing the Laurels of Victory Stands & Shoots as a charge reaction, causing one or more unsaved wounds, are those unsaved wounds each worth 2 combat result points?
answer: No.

name: If my Battle Standard Bearer refuses a challenge and retires from combat, can other units still benefit from their "Hold Your Ground" special rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If my Battle Standard Bearer refuses a challenge and retires from combat, can other units still benefit from their "Hold Your Ground" special rule?
answer: Not whilst that character's unit is still engaged with the model that issued the challenge. Battle standards are somewhat less inspirational whilst being used as a blanket to hide beneath.

name: Does a spell cast on a Regimental Unit affect its Detachments, or vice versa?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a spell cast on a Regimental Unit affect its Detachments, or vice versa?
answer: No, spells that affect a unit only affect the unit they are cast on.

name: Can a Troll Hag be the General of an Orc and Goblin Tribes army made using the Grand Army composition list?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Troll Hag be the General of an Orc and Goblin Tribes army made using the Grand Army composition list?
answer: No. A Troll Hag is a Rare choice in the Orc and Goblin Tribes Grand Army composition list. She can, however, be the General of a Troll Horde.

name: If a model is required to make a Leadership test using its own Leadership characteristic, can it use the Leadership of a different model, the General for example, instead?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a model is required to make a Leadership test using its own Leadership characteristic, can it use the Leadership of a different model, the General for example, instead?
answer: No. When the rules state a model must make a Leadership test using its own Leadership, it must use its own Leadership.

name: What happens to a Fanatic that ends its movement within the Command range of one or more models with the Hostile Terrain special rule?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: What happens to a Fanatic that ends its movement within the Command range of one or more models with the Hostile Terrain special rule?
answer: The Fanatic must make a Dangerous Terrain test. Rather than creating an area of dangerous terrain, Hostile Terrain represents a model laying traps and creating hazards, causing enemy models to make a dangerous terrain test should they end their movement within that area.

name: How many Remains in Play spells can I attempt to dispel during my Conjuration sub-phase?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How many Remains in Play spells can I attempt to dispel during my Conjuration sub-phase?
answer: As many as you wish, or, perhaps more accurately, as many as your opponent has in play and as many as you are able.

name: If I upgrade a unit of Boar Boyz to be Big 'Uns, do their War Boars benefit as well?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If I upgrade a unit of Boar Boyz to be Big 'Uns, do their War Boars benefit as well?
answer: Yes. Note that the Strength characteristic of Big 'Uns is modified whilst they are engaged in combat, and that Impact Hits are made using the model's unmodified Strength characteristic.

name: If a Troll Hag purchases a magic weapon, are any of its special rules conferred onto Motherly Love attacks?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: If a Troll Hag purchases a magic weapon, are any of its special rules conferred onto Motherly Love attacks?
answer: No. If she attacks normally, she can use her magic weapon. If she makes a Motherly Love attack, she does not use her magic weapon.

name: When a character takes a chariot as a mount, do they replace one of the crew?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a character takes a chariot as a mount, do they replace one of the crew?
answer: No. The points you pay for a chariot include its crew, whether that chariot is included in your army on its own or as a mount for a character. However, in reality it might prove difficult to fit a character and the full complement of crew into the chariot model itself. If so, it is perfectly acceptable to remove one or more of the crew models to make space for the character. In such cases, we can think of the missing crew model's profile(s) as a way of representing that the beasts pulling a character's chariot are bound to be bigger, stronger and better trained than normal.

name: If a unit charged by two or more enemy units Counter Charges one of them, it may end its movement presenting a different arc to the other charging unit(s). Do those units resolve their charges against those arcs?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit charged by two or more enemy units Counter Charges one of them, it may end its movement presenting a different arc to the other charging unit(s). Do those units resolve their charges against those arcs?
answer: Yes. When a unit Counter Charges, it risks exposing its flanks or rear to the enemy, such are the perils of bravado.

name: Does the Griffon Standard allow a unit to claim a higher Rank Bonus than normal for its troop type?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Does the Griffon Standard allow a unit to claim a higher Rank Bonus than normal for its troop type?
answer: Yes. As stated in the item's rules, a unit carrying the Griffon Standard can claim a Rank Bonus of +2 for each extra rank behind the first, rather than the usual +1, meaning a unit with two extra ranks could claim a Rank Bonus of +4, rather than the usual +2.

name: My unit charges an enemy unit that is defending a low linear obstacle, making base contact with the obstacle rather than the unit. Can my unit make Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: My unit charges an enemy unit that is defending a low linear obstacle, making base contact with the obstacle rather than the unit. Can my unit make Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks?
answer: No. Models must be in base contact with the enemy to make Impact Hits or Stomp Attacks.

name: When a Goblin Hewer shoots using the Whirling Death special rule, does it add a D3 modifier to the number of shots rolled for each rank or file, or just a single D3 modifier?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: When a Goblin Hewer shoots using the Whirling Death special rule, does it add a D3 modifier to the number of shots rolled for each rank or file, or just a single D3 modifier?
answer: Just a single D3 modifier. You do not get an extra D3 per rank of file.

name: If a unit of enemy infantry charges a unit with the Master Rune of Hesitation, can it make use of the Press of Battle rule to fight in two ranks?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit of enemy infantry charges a unit with the Master Rune of Hesitation, can it make use of the Press of Battle rule to fight in two ranks?
answer: No. Although the Rune prevents a charging enemy unit from using certain weapons or special rules, the unit has still charged, and thus cannot make use of the Press of Battle rule.

name: Can a war machine shoot using the Ballistic Skill of a character that has joined it?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a war machine shoot using the Ballistic Skill of a character that has joined it?
answer: No. Because a war machine and its crew are treated as a single model, you must use the crew's BS.

name: Some single use special rules and magic items require a Leadership test to use. Do they count as having been used even if this test is failed?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some single use special rules and magic items require a Leadership test to use. Do they count as having been used even if this test is failed?
answer: Yes.

name: When a unit performs a manoeuvre, can its front edge pass through another unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit performs a manoeuvre, can its front edge pass through another unit?
answer: No. The only exception to this is when a unit pivots.

name: Some chariots are equipped with large scale missile weapons such as bolt throwers. Who shoots such weapons;  the crew, the beasts that draw the chariot, or the chariot itself?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some chariots are equipped with large scale missile weapons such as bolt throwers. Who shoots such weapons;  the crew, the beasts that draw the chariot, or the chariot itself?
answer: Missile weapons mounted on chariots (or howdahs) are shot by the crew, using their Ballistic Skill.

name: If a character whose mount has a different sized base to the models in a unit making up a Lance formation wishes to join that unit, where should I place the model?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a character whose mount has a different sized base to the models in a unit making up a Lance formation wishes to join that unit, where should I place the model?
answer: The Lance formation offers a bit more flexibility than other formations. For example, it is perfectly acceptable to place a character such as a Duke or a Baron at the front of the Lance should you wish. In the case of a Handmaiden of the Lady, the Shield of the Lady special rule allows such models to be placed  at the rear of such units. Alternatively, they can be placed within the unit. In this case, the extra base size will make very little difference to the shape of the unit.

name: How does the Spectral Doppelganger spell interact with weapons that have either the Strike First or Strike Last special rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How does the Spectral Doppelganger spell interact with weapons that have either the Strike First or Strike Last special rule?
answer: The spell is cast at the Wizard's Initiative, meaning attacks generated by the spell are made at the Wizard's Initiative. However, if the casting Wizard wishes to use a weapon with the Strike Last special rule, those attacks are made at Initiative 1.

name: Do potions affect a model's mount?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do potions affect a model's mount?
answer: No.

name: When shooting a Doom Diver catapult, the template can be moved D3" after scattering. Can I use this to move the template so that it hits an enemy character that is within 3" of a unit, a character that could not usually be targeted by shooting?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: When shooting a Doom Diver catapult, the template can be moved D3" after scattering. Can I use this to move the template so that it hits an enemy character that is within 3" of a unit, a character that could not usually be targeted by shooting?
answer: Yes. The template must be moved the full distance rolled on the D3 but, if that allows you to position it to hit an enemy character, you may do so. Sneaky Goblin Doom Divers steer their flight in an attempt to hit the best possible target, so this is completely in character.

name: Is there a maximum beyond which characteristics cannot be modified?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Is there a maximum beyond which characteristics cannot be modified?
answer: Unless a rule specifies a maximum, no.
