name: Some war machines can have additional models attached to them, such as the Orc Bully. If the war machine is destroyed, are any attached models also destroyed?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some war machines can have additional models attached to them, such as the Orc Bully. If the war machine is destroyed, are any attached models also destroyed?
answer: Yes.

name: If a unit whose troop type is 'infantry' that has been joined by a character whose troop type is 'cavalry' charges an enemy unit with the Counter Charge special rule, can that unit Counter Charge?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit whose troop type is 'infantry' that has been joined by a character whose troop type is 'cavalry' charges an enemy unit with the Counter Charge special rule, can that unit Counter Charge?
answer: No. A unit's troop type doesn't change when joined by a character.

name: Can the size of a range 'self' spell's aura of effect be increased by the Idol of Gork?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can the size of a range 'self' spell's aura of effect be increased by the Idol of Gork?
answer: No. The aura of effect of a range 'self' spell is not its range.

name: A character mounted on a ridden monster or a chariot can choose to use their own or their mount's armour value, whichever is better. If the character wears magic armour but I choose to use the mount's armour value, can I still claim other benefits conferred by the magic armour?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: A character mounted on a ridden monster or a chariot can choose to use their own or their mount's armour value, whichever is better. If the character wears magic armour but I choose to use the mount's armour value, can I still claim other benefits conferred by the magic armour?
answer: No. You must use a magic item fully or not at all.

name: If a unit of enemy infantry charges a unit with the Master Rune of Hesitation, does it gain the Initiative bonus for charging?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit of enemy infantry charges a unit with the Master Rune of Hesitation, does it gain the Initiative bonus for charging?
answer: Yes. The Master Rune of Hesitation only prevents the use of certain weapons and special rules.

name: Can a Close Order unit of just one model claim the Close Order combat result bonus?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Close Order unit of just one model claim the Close Order combat result bonus?
answer: Yes, provided it has a high enough Unit Strength (as per the errata on page 1). A unit of just one model is still a unit.

name: When a unit wheels, is it acceptable for a rear corner to pass through another unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit wheels, is it acceptable for a rear corner to pass through another unit?
answer: Yes, provided the unit does not end its movement 'on top' of another unit or within 1" of an enemy unit.

name: When a unit in Lance formation is engaged in combat, every model on the outside counts as being in base contact. How many enemy models count as being in base contact with the Lance?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit in Lance formation is engaged in combat, every model on the outside counts as being in base contact. How many enemy models count as being in base contact with the Lance?
answer: Every model within the first fighting rank that is within a number of inches equal to its Movement characteristic of the Lance. In the case of enemy regular or heavy infantry, the second fighting rank is not in base contact – there is another rank in the way. When a Lance charges, it pierces deep into the enemy formation, pushing warriors back and causing the enemy lines to close around it. It’s very hard to show this on the table though, hence the abstraction. Note that this can create an area of ‘dead ground’ between a Lance and a unit it is engaged in combat with. If any enemy units are caught within this, refer to the rules covering ‘accidental contact’ on page 131 of the Warhammer: the Old World rulebook.

name: If the Scroll of Transmogrification is successfully used against a Wizard mounted on a ridden monster (a Dragon, for example), what happens to the mount?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If the Scroll of Transmogrification is successfully used against a Wizard mounted on a ridden monster (a Dragon, for example), what happens to the mount?
answer: The Wizard is transmogrified, but not their mount. In the example given, you would be left with a Frog riding a Dragon.

name: What happens to a Remains in Play spell that is already in effect if the Wizard that cast it attempts to cast it again?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens to a Remains in Play spell that is already in effect if the Wizard that cast it attempts to cast it again?
answer: It ends immediately.

name: What happens to a unit that Gives Ground whilst within 2" of the edge of the battlefield?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens to a unit that Gives Ground whilst within 2" of the edge of the battlefield?
answer: Should any part of a unit cross beyond the edge of the battlefield whilst it is Giving Ground, the entire unit is removed from play and counts as having been destroyed.

name: All Goblins Fear Elves. If a unit of Elves causes Fear, does this lead them to cause Terror in Goblins?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: All Goblins Fear Elves. If a unit of Elves causes Fear, does this lead them to cause Terror in Goblins?
answer: No.

name: If I have a character with an armour value of 6+ mounted on a chariot with an armour value of 4+, for example, am I obliged to use the chariot's armour value?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If I have a character with an armour value of 6+ mounted on a chariot with an armour value of 4+, for example, am I obliged to use the chariot's armour value?
answer: No, you choose whichever armour value is better. However, allowing for magical armour and other special rules, 'better' is somewhat subjective. Obviously an armour value of 4+ is better than one of 6+ but there is often more to deciding which armour to use than just the armour value it offers, many types of magic armour confer additional bonuses that make them better. Basically, when the rules say 'choose', they are giving you a choice.

name: How far does a model with the Random Movement special rule move when making a Counter Charge?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How far does a model with the Random Movement special rule move when making a Counter Charge?
answer: As stated in the Counter Charge special rule, a model that makes a Counter Charge moves D3+1".

name: Can a unit arrayed in Close Order or Open Order formation be one model wide?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit arrayed in Close Order or Open Order formation be one model wide?
answer: Yes.

name: If a unit will have to wheel before or during a charge, do I have to allow for that when declaring and measuring the charge?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit will have to wheel before or during a charge, do I have to allow for that when declaring and measuring the charge?
answer: Yes. Wheeling during a charge is part of the charge move. The free wheel to align once contact is made is free.

name: Is a charging Lance blunted if an enemy unit counter charges it?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Is a charging Lance blunted if an enemy unit counter charges it?
answer: No. As both units are charging, the clause that a Lance is blunted if it is charged and does not counter charge is negated.

name: Can a model wearing armour or carrying a shield cast a Bound spell?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a model wearing armour or carrying a shield cast a Bound spell?
answer: Yes.

name: What happens if a Scroll of Transmogrification is used on a Bound spell?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a Scroll of Transmogrification is used on a Bound spell?
answer: After testing against the item's Power Level (in place of its Level of Wizardry), it may be turned into an inanimate frog, rendering it useless until it turns back. During each Start of Turn sub-phase, you may roll a D6 for each transmogrified item in your army to see if it returns to normal, as described on page 343.

name: If some of my opponent’s attacks cause multiple Wounds to be lost per unsaved wound they cause, in what order are those unsaved wounds applied to my unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If some of my opponent’s attacks cause multiple Wounds to be lost per unsaved wound they cause, in what order are those unsaved wounds applied to my unit?
answer: Apply unsaved wounds to a unit one at a time and one model at a time. When a model is reduced to zero Wounds on its profile, it is removed from play as a casualty (as described on page 102). If a model loses more Wounds than it has on its profile, the excess wounds are lost – they do not spill over onto other models in the same unit. With this in mind, apply unsaved wounds that cause models to lose all of their remaining Wounds first (Killing Blows and Monster Slaying Blows, for example), then unsaved wounds that cause multiple Wounds to be lost (Multiple Wounds (X), for example), then, finally, unsaved wounds that cause a single Wound to be lost.

name: Can a unit with the Finest Warhorses special rule re-roll the dice when using Swiftstride?
source_document: Official Forces of Fantasy FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit with the Finest Warhorses special rule re-roll the dice when using Swiftstride?
answer: No. Finest Warhorses allows a unit to re-roll its Charge roll, but the D6 rolled when using Swiftstride is not part of the charge roll; it is rolled to give a modifier to the result of the Charge roll.

name: Can the Glowy Green Amulet be used by a character that isn't a Wizard?
source_document: Official Ravening Hordes FAQ & Errata
question: Can the Glowy Green Amulet be used by a character that isn't a Wizard?
answer: Yes. The Glowy Green Amulet is a talisman, meaning any character that can take magic items can take it. However, if they wish to use it against an enemy spell, they do so instead of a Wizardly dispel attempt being made. This means that you cannot also attempt a Wizardly dispel against a spell you use the Glowy Green Amulet against, even if the Glowy Green Amulet explodes.

name: Can a Wizard with a Lore Familiar choose one of their faction's signature spells instead of the signature spell of their chosen lore?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wizard with a Lore Familiar choose one of their faction's signature spells instead of the signature spell of their chosen lore?
answer: No.

name: What happens to a unit that cannot Give Ground due to the presence of another unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens to a unit that cannot Give Ground due to the presence of another unit?
answer: The unit's movement stops immediately, as if it were surrounded.

name: If a character mounted on a ridden monster or chariot carries a shield, does that improve the mount's armour value?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a character mounted on a ridden monster or chariot carries a shield, does that improve the mount's armour value?
answer: No, a shield is carried by the character, not the mount.

name: Does a unit that has to declare a charge due to being Frenzied or Impetuous have to do so if a friendly unit of Skirmishers lies between it and a potential charge target, obstructing its movement?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a unit that has to declare a charge due to being Frenzied or Impetuous have to do so if a friendly unit of Skirmishers lies between it and a potential charge target, obstructing its movement?
answer: If there is a chance of the Skirmishers moving so that they are no longer an obstruction (if they declare a charge, for example), yes. Otherwise, no.

name: Can a Drilled unit redress the ranks before Giving Ground?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Drilled unit redress the ranks before Giving Ground?
answer: Yes. A unit that Gives Ground is not a fleeing unit. Note that, as mentioned previously, should any part of a unit cross beyond the edge of the battlefield whilst it is Giving Ground, the entire unit is removed from play and counts as having been destroyed. It is intentional that a Drilled unit might be able to avoid this fate.

name: Can a Wizard attempt to dispel a Bound spell by attempting a Wizardly dispel?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wizard attempt to dispel a Bound spell by attempting a Wizardly dispel?
answer: If the Wizard is within range of the model casting the Bound spell and not otherwise prevented from attempting a Wizardly dispel, yes. As stated on page 109, you may attempt to dispel a Bound spell as usual.

name: Although it cannot make a charge move, a unit in Marching Column can declare a charge. Why is this?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Although it cannot make a charge move, a unit in Marching Column can declare a charge. Why is this?
answer: There are several reasons. Firstly, a Drilled unit that declares a charge whilst in Marching Column can freely redress its ranks to adopt Combat Order after determining its charge range, but before moving, thus allowing it to make a charge move. Secondly, units that are obliged to declare a charge in certain circumstances (those that are Frenzied or Impetuous, for example) must do so even whilst in Marching Column. If they cannot make the charge move, they don't move at all and the charge is failed. This prevents Marching Column being used to avoid declaring compulsory charges. Alternatively, there might be a psychological advantage to declaring a charge with a Marching Column. For example, the unit might cause Terror, or the charge target might already be fleeing. Of course, it isn't easy to set up situations where such tactics can be used and therein lies the challenge.

name: How many spells does a Wizard with an Arcane Familiar know? For example, does a Level 3 Wizard know three spells from two Lores (two from one and one from the other), or six spells, three from each Lore?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How many spells does a Wizard with an Arcane Familiar know? For example, does a Level 3 Wizard know three spells from two Lores (two from one and one from the other), or six spells, three from each Lore?
answer: An Arcane Familiar does not increase the number of spells a Wizard knows, it simply allows them to know spells from two Lores. Thus a Level 3 Wizard would know three spells from two Lores.

name: Can an Evasive unit use that special rule whilst engaged in combat?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can an Evasive unit use that special rule whilst engaged in combat?
answer: No.

name: If a unit with the Press of Battle rule has two fighting ranks, can I remove casualties from the second rank?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit with the Press of Battle rule has two fighting ranks, can I remove casualties from the second rank?
answer: No. Models in the first fighting rank, the rank in base contact with the enemy, fall first as casualties.

name: What happens if a unit that wishes to make a pursuit or overrun move cannot move due to the presence of other units?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a unit that wishes to make a pursuit or overrun move cannot move due to the presence of other units?
answer: It may happen that it is simply impossible for a pursuing or overrunning unit to move without it ending up 'on top' of another unit. In such cases, the unit does not move, but is considered to have made a pursuit or overrun move.

name: Can a Wizard with a physical attribute that 'counts as' a type of armour (such as a Treeman Ancient's arboreal armour) make Casting or Dispel rolls?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wizard with a physical attribute that 'counts as' a type of armour (such as a Treeman Ancient's arboreal armour) make Casting or Dispel rolls?
answer: Yes. Whilst such attributes are as protective as a suit of armour, such models do not wear armour.

name: Does the presence in a unit of a character whose base takes up the space of two or more models in two or more ranks reduce that unit’s rank bonus?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does the presence in a unit of a character whose base takes up the space of two or more models in two or more ranks reduce that unit’s rank bonus?
answer: No.

name: If a unit contains models of two different troop types, how do I determine its maximum rank bonus?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit contains models of two different troop types, how do I determine its maximum rank bonus?
answer: In such cases, the troop type of the majority of the models determines the unit’s maximum rank bonus.

name: Do effects that modify a model's Movement characteristic also modify how far a model with the Fly (X) special rule can fly?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do effects that modify a model's Movement characteristic also modify how far a model with the Fly (X) special rule can fly?
answer: Yes. If a model has the Fly (X) special rule, the number given in brackets is essentially a second Movement characteristic. Any effect that modifies one will modify both.

name: Some models can be found in one faction's army list, but can be included in an army made using a composition list belonging to a different faction (Dragon Ogre Shaggoths, or units that can be included as Mercenaries, for example). What lists of magic items do such models have access to?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some models can be found in one faction's army list, but can be included in an army made using a composition list belonging to a different faction (Dragon Ogre Shaggoths, or units that can be included as Mercenaries, for example). What lists of magic items do such models have access to?
answer: Models can purchase magic items from the list of common magic items in the Warhammer: the Old World rulebook, or from their own faction's list of magic items. A Dragon Ogre Shaggoth, for example, can purchase items from the common or Beastmen Brayherds magic item lists.

name: If a unit completely destroys its enemy in combat and makes an overrun move, can it attempt to reform after moving?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit completely destroys its enemy in combat and makes an overrun move, can it attempt to reform after moving?
answer: Unless it pursued into a fresh enemy, yes. A unit that overruns makes a normal pursuit move and, since its enemy has already been wiped out, it may attempt to reform as if it had run down its foe, as described on page 129.

name: A Clumsy unit cannot be joined by a character that is not also Clumsy, but can a Clumsy character join a unit that is not Clumsy?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: A Clumsy unit cannot be joined by a character that is not also Clumsy, but can a Clumsy character join a unit that is not Clumsy?
answer: Yes.

name: Does a Wizard that has joined a unit need to be in base contact with an enemy model to cast an Assailment spell, or do they only need to be within the fighting rank?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a Wizard that has joined a unit need to be in base contact with an enemy model to cast an Assailment spell, or do they only need to be within the fighting rank?
answer: They only need to be within the fighting rank.

name: If a Drilled unit in Marching Column has to declare a charge due to being Frenzied or Impetuous, can it choose not to use Drilled to redress the ranks and adopt combat order?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Drilled unit in Marching Column has to declare a charge due to being Frenzied or Impetuous, can it choose not to use Drilled to redress the ranks and adopt combat order?
answer: If it is able to redress the ranks (i.e., if there is space for it to do so), no. A unit that is obliged to charge must endeavour to make use of any special rules it has in order to charge. The unit just really wants to charge, and it'll play this game without you if it has to!

name: If a Wizard declines a challenge and retires from combat, does their unit lose the benefits of any range 'Self' spells that Wizard has cast?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Wizard declines a challenge and retires from combat, does their unit lose the benefits of any range 'Self' spells that Wizard has cast?
answer: Yes.

name: On page 157 it states that units that pursue and catch an enemy that Fell Back in Good Order count as having charged during the next turn, but what exactly does this mean?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: On page 157 it states that units that pursue and catch an enemy that Fell Back in Good Order count as having charged during the next turn, but what exactly does this mean?
answer: Exactly what it says – the pursuing unit counts as having charged its enemy during the next turn of the game, when the combat is fought, even though the unit will not actually have moved during that turn. This means rules and weapons that can only be used by charging models can be used, that Initiative modifiers for charging are granted (based on the distance the unit pursued), and so forth.

name: Does a unit that is joined by a character of a different troop type gain the rules associated with the character’s troop type?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a unit that is joined by a character of a different troop type gain the rules associated with the character’s troop type?
answer: No.

name: How does a Close Order unit of just one model act, is it still a Close Order formation, or does it act like a Skirmisher?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How does a Close Order unit of just one model act, is it still a Close Order formation, or does it act like a Skirmisher?
answer: A Close Order unit always acts as such, even if it only contains a single model.

name: If I want to charge an enemy unit that is a Large Target with a unit of mine that is in its front arc (for example) but cannot because the enemy unit is already engaged in its front arc, can I instead charge its flank?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If I want to charge an enemy unit that is a Large Target with a unit of mine that is in its front arc (for example) but cannot because the enemy unit is already engaged in its front arc, can I instead charge its flank?
answer: No. Sometimes a charge isn't possible.

name: Can a model with two versions of the Fly (X) special rule combine both and fly further?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a model with two versions of the Fly (X) special rule combine both and fly further?
answer: No. A model that has more than one version of the Fly (X) special rule essentially has two Movement characteristics it can use when flying. Of these, you may use the best.

name: If a unit with the Fly (X) special rule moves over a magical vortex that counts as dangerous terrain, is it affected by it?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit with the Fly (X) special rule moves over a magical vortex that counts as dangerous terrain, is it affected by it?
answer: Yes. Magical vortexes are considered tall enough to affect even models that are flying high above the battlefield.

name: If a unit with the Fly (X) special rule is engaged in combat, can it use its 'Fly Movement' characteristic when determining which models are close enough to models within the enemy unit to fight or make supporting attacks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit with the Fly (X) special rule is engaged in combat, can it use its 'Fly Movement' characteristic when determining which models are close enough to models within the enemy unit to fight or make supporting attacks?
answer: No. Models with the Fly (X) special rule only fly whilst moving. In other words, such models are considered to have landed on the ground whilst the fighting takes place, and must use their given Movement characteristic to determine if they are close enough to the enemy to fight.

name: Can a character who is within the fighting rank of their unit but not in base contact with the enemy move through the ranks so that they are?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a character who is within the fighting rank of their unit but not in base contact with the enemy move through the ranks so that they are?
answer: No. Characters can move through the ranks into the fighting rank, but cannot move within the fighting rank.

name: Does a unit count as being obscured when some of the models within it are behind others?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Does a unit count as being obscured when some of the models within it are behind others?
answer: No. A unit cannot be obscured from the enemy by itself.

name: If a single model unit straddles two arcs of an enemy unit it wishes to charge, is it always considered to be in the arc that gives the least advantage?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a single model unit straddles two arcs of an enemy unit it wishes to charge, is it always considered to be in the arc that gives the least advantage?
answer: No, this is only the case when you cannot determine which arc of an enemy unit a charging unit is within. If it is clear that the majority of a charging unit is within one particular arc, even if that unit consists of only one model, it will charge into that arc.

name: Can different saves be combined together to improve their save value? For example, if a model has two Ward saves, can they be combined to give a single Ward save with a better Warding value?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can different saves be combined together to improve their save value? For example, if a model has two Ward saves, can they be combined to give a single Ward save with a better Warding value?
answer: No, values given as a target number cannot be combined to lower the target number. If a model has two or more Ward saves, as per the example, simply use the best.

name: Can a character be placed amongst a unit's command group?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a character be placed amongst a unit's command group?
answer: Yes.

name: If a model with a split profile has the Impact Hits (X) special rule, do all parts of the model cause Impact Hits?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a model with a split profile has the Impact Hits (X) special rule, do all parts of the model cause Impact Hits?
answer: No. Impact Hits are only made by a mount, not by its rider(s). In the case of a chariot, Impact Hits are made by the chariot itself, not by the beasts that draw it. If a rider has their own Impact Hits (X) special rule, these are made separately by the rider.

name: If a unit charges an enemy that is defending a low linear obstacle, can it stop on top of the obstacle?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit charges an enemy that is defending a low linear obstacle, can it stop on top of the obstacle?
answer: No. As stated on page 270, the charging unit moves into base contact with the obstacle.

name: If there is no space for a character in the front rank of a unit with the Press of Battle rule, can they join the second rank?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If there is no space for a character in the front rank of a unit with the Press of Battle rule, can they join the second rank?
answer: No, characters must be placed in the front rank. If this is not possible, they retire to the rear.

name: Impact Hits and Stomp Attacks cannot be directed against a character that has joined a unit unless that unit has a Unit Strength of five or less. Does this limitation also apply to unit champions?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Impact Hits and Stomp Attacks cannot be directed against a character that has joined a unit unless that unit has a Unit Strength of five or less. Does this limitation also apply to unit champions?
answer: Yes. Whilst unit champions are not characters, they function like characters in many ways, and this is one. Impact Hits and Stomp Attacks are indiscriminate, and targeting specific models, like unit champions, is not possible unless there are fewer than five rank and file models in the unit.

name: When a unit redirects a charge, can it charge a unit that lies beyond its maximum possible charge range?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit redirects a charge, can it charge a unit that lies beyond its maximum possible charge range?
answer: No.

name: If a unit with Frenzy or Impetuous has two movement characteristics (for example, if it can also Fly), does it have to use the greater when determining if it must declare a charge?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit with Frenzy or Impetuous has two movement characteristics (for example, if it can also Fly), does it have to use the greater when determining if it must declare a charge?
answer: If it is able to use the greater, then yes, it must (tactically, you might not want it to, but a Frenzied Lord of Chaos on a mighty Dragon doesn't care about your tactics!).

name: Can characters displace command group models into the second rank of a unit with the Press of Battle rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can characters displace command group models into the second rank of a unit with the Press of Battle rule?
answer: No, command group models must always be in the front rank of their unit.

name: When a unit enters the battle as reinforcements, is there a limit to how far onto the battlefield models can be placed?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit enters the battle as reinforcements, is there a limit to how far onto the battlefield models can be placed?
answer: Yes. The unit is considered to have moved onto the battlefield. Therefore, no model can be placed more than twice its Movement characteristic from the edge of the battlefield its unit enters from.

name: Can a Wizard cast a Conveyance spell during a turn in which they charged?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a Wizard cast a Conveyance spell during a turn in which they charged?
answer: No.

name: If a character is in the back rank of a unit due to there not being enough space for them in the front rank, do they confer any bonuses onto the unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a character is in the back rank of a unit due to there not being enough space for them in the front rank, do they confer any bonuses onto the unit?
answer: Unless they have a special rule stating otherwise, no.

name: If a unit that is subject to Frenzy becomes subject to Frenzy again, does it get +2 Attacks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a unit that is subject to Frenzy becomes subject to Frenzy again, does it get +2 Attacks?
answer: No. A unit is either Frenzied or it isn't, multiple instances of Frenzy are not cumulative.

name: If a Warband character (as described on page 180) joins a Warband with a Rank Bonus of one or more, does the modifier to their Leadership characteristic increase their Command range?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Warband character (as described on page 180) joins a Warband with a Rank Bonus of one or more, does the modifier to their Leadership characteristic increase their Command range?
answer: Yes.

name: 'War beasts' and 'swarms' Fear models with the Flaming Attacks special rule. Do 'war beasts' and 'swarms' that have the Immune to Psychology special rule Fear models that have the Flaming Attacks special rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: 'War beasts' and 'swarms' Fear models with the Flaming Attacks special rule. Do 'war beasts' and 'swarms' that have the Immune to Psychology special rule Fear models that have the Flaming Attacks special rule?
answer: Yes. Even though models that cause Fear are normally immune to it, the rule that Swarms and War beasts Fear models with Flaming Attacks is an exception called out in those troop types' unique rules.

name: If some models in a unit cause Fear but others do not, do I use the Unit Strength of the whole unit or just that of the models that cause Fear to determine if an enemy unit has a lower Unit Strength?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If some models in a unit cause Fear but others do not, do I use the Unit Strength of the whole unit or just that of the models that cause Fear to determine if an enemy unit has a lower Unit Strength?
answer: Count only the Unit Strength of the models that cause Fear.

name: My unit wishes to shoot at an enemy lone character. However, an enemy unit that is more than 3" away from that character presents a closer target. Can I shoot at the lone character?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: My unit wishes to shoot at an enemy lone character. However, an enemy unit that is more than 3" away from that character presents a closer target. Can I shoot at the lone character?
answer: Yes. The rule that a lone character cannot be shot at unless they are the closest target only extends to units they are within 3" of. If a unit is more than 3" from the character, it does not protect them, regardless of how close it is to the enemy.

name: Units with the Motley Crew special rule use the armour value of the 'majority' of models. What if there is an even number of different armour values?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Units with the Motley Crew special rule use the armour value of the 'majority' of models. What if there is an even number of different armour values?
answer: In such cases, use the better armour value (not including the armour value of any characters or champions that have purchased magic items or unique upgrades to improve their armour).

name: Can a character that has joined a unit of models of a different troop type be targeted by enemy shooting? For example, if a character whose troop type is 'light chariot' joins a unit of 'regular infantry', can they be targeted?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a character that has joined a unit of models of a different troop type be targeted by enemy shooting? For example, if a character whose troop type is 'light chariot' joins a unit of 'regular infantry', can they be targeted?
answer: Yes. What's more, they don't get a "Look Out, Sir!" save either.

name: If a character joins a unit that carries a magic standard, do its effects extend to the character?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a character joins a unit that carries a magic standard, do its effects extend to the character?
answer: Yes. When a character joins a unit they become part of that unit, so magic standards that affect the unit affect them as well.

name: What happens if a unit with the Move through Cover special rule is joined by a character without it?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a unit with the Move through Cover special rule is joined by a character without it?
answer: Units move at the speed of their slowest model. Therefore, whilst the unit would suffer no penalty for moving through difficult or dangerous terrain, the character would, potentially reducing how far the entire unit can move.

name: If a Warband character joins a unit that isn't a Warband, does that unit's Rank Bonus modify their Leadership characteristic?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a Warband character joins a unit that isn't a Warband, does that unit's Rank Bonus modify their Leadership characteristic?
answer: No.

name: What happens if a weapon is subject to both the Ponderous and Quick Shot special rules?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: What happens if a weapon is subject to both the Ponderous and Quick Shot special rules?
answer: The rules effectively cancel one another out, meaning the weapon would suffer a -1 To Hit modifier for Moving and Shooting.

name: Can a unit with the Random Movement special rule move around or past an enemy unit, out of one arc and into another, before making contact with that unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit with the Random Movement special rule move around or past an enemy unit, out of one arc and into another, before making contact with that unit?
answer: No. Whilst units that move randomly do not declare charges, if you wish to move one into contact with an enemy unit, it must fulfil the same criteria as any other charging unit during its movement, as detailed on page 126.

name: Cannon do not directly target enemy models; they target a point on the ground. How does this work with Targeting Lone characters? Can a cannon be fired in such a way as to hit a Lone character that would normally be protected from shooting by their proximity to a friendly unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Cannon do not directly target enemy models; they target a point on the ground. How does this work with Targeting Lone characters? Can a cannon be fired in such a way as to hit a Lone character that would normally be protected from shooting by their proximity to a friendly unit?
answer: The purpose of the Targeting Lone Characters rule is to protect characters from enemy shooting, even from shooting that does not follow the usual rules (though it does not protect them from templates). Therefore, and in the spirit of this rule, unless they are the closest target to the cannon, an enemy character that is within 3" of a friendly unit that contains five or more models of the same troop type cannot be struck by a cannonball, even if their base lies directly under the path of the bouncing cannonball. However, if a Lone character's base lies directly underneath the strike point of a cannonball, they are hit.

name: How far does a unit with the Random Movement special rule move when Giving Ground?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: How far does a unit with the Random Movement special rule move when Giving Ground?
answer: All units move 2" when Giving Ground.

name: Some magic weapons or special rules allow specific models within a unit to be targeted. Do such attacks ignore the "Look Out, Sir!" rule?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Some magic weapons or special rules allow specific models within a unit to be targeted. Do such attacks ignore the "Look Out, Sir!" rule?
answer: No.

name: Can a unit that deploys using the Scouts special rule make a Vanguard move?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a unit that deploys using the Scouts special rule make a Vanguard move?
answer: No.

name: If a named character is equipped with a mundane weapon such as a lance or cavalry spear and a magic weapon, can they choose to use the lance or cavalry spear, or must they use their magic weapon?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a named character is equipped with a mundane weapon such as a lance or cavalry spear and a magic weapon, can they choose to use the lance or cavalry spear, or must they use their magic weapon?
answer: They must use their magic weapon. However, some named characters will carry a 'back-up' weapon which they can use should their magic weapon be destroyed.

name: Are units that deploy using the Scouts special rule counted when determining which player finished deploying their army first?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Are units that deploy using the Scouts special rule counted when determining which player finished deploying their army first?
answer: Yes.

name: If a model with a split profile has the Stomp Attacks (X) special rule, do all parts of the model make Stomp Attacks?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a model with a split profile has the Stomp Attacks (X) special rule, do all parts of the model make Stomp Attacks?
answer: No. Stomp attacks are only made by a mount, not by its rider(s). In the case of a chariot, Stomp Attacks are made by the beasts that draw it or, if there are no beasts, by the chariot itself.

name: When a unit that does not have the Stupidity special rule is joined by a character that does, the unit becomes subject to the special rule. What happens if the character leaves the unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: When a unit that does not have the Stupidity special rule is joined by a character that does, the unit becomes subject to the special rule. What happens if the character leaves the unit?
answer: The unit ceases to be subject to Stupidity the moment the character leaves the unit.

name: Swiftstride enables a model to move further during a charge move than its maximum possible charge range. Why is this?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Swiftstride enables a model to move further during a charge move than its maximum possible charge range. Why is this?
answer: Because models with this special rule delight in running down cowards who flee before a charge!

name: Do models that cause Terror cause it in models that cause Fear?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Do models that cause Terror cause it in models that cause Fear?
answer: No. Models that cause Terror cause Fear in models with the Fear special rule, even though such models are normally immune to Fear.

name: If the winning side of a combat includes one or more models that cause Terror, do models on the losing side that cause Fear or Terror suffer the -1 modifier to their Leadership?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If the winning side of a combat includes one or more models that cause Terror, do models on the losing side that cause Fear or Terror suffer the -1 modifier to their Leadership?
answer: No. Because models that cause Fear or Terror aren't terrified by Terror causing enemies, they do not suffer the effects of Terror.

name: Are Wounds lost as a result of a model with Timmmberrr! falling on a unit counted towards the enemy’s combat result score?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Are Wounds lost as a result of a model with Timmmberrr! falling on a unit counted towards the enemy’s combat result score?
answer: Yes.

name: If a character without the Vanguard special rule joins a unit with it during deployment, can the character make a Vanguard move with the unit?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: If a character without the Vanguard special rule joins a unit with it during deployment, can the character make a Vanguard move with the unit?
answer: No. What’s more, if the unit is formed, it will not be able to make a Vanguard move; the presence of a character without the Vanguard special rule within its ranks prevents it. However, if the unit is in Skirmish formation, it can make its Vanguard move as normal, leaving the character behind.

name: Can a character without the Veteran special rule that has joined a unit with the Veteran special rule benefit from it when attempting to use a special rule that requires them to make a Leadership test (Rallying Cry, for example)?
source_document: Official Warhammer: The Old World FAQ & Errata
question: Can a character without the Veteran special rule that has joined a unit with the Veteran special rule benefit from it when attempting to use a special rule that requires them to make a Leadership test (Rallying Cry, for example)?
answer: No. When a character attempts to use a special rule that requires them to make a Leadership test, they must use their own Leadership characteristic and, unless specifically stated otherwise, cannot use any additional special rules they themselves do not have.
